comment_text.sn35.151:1.1: The Vinaya establishes that a newly ordained monastic should live in dependence on a mentor (uppajjhāya) and/or a “tutor” (ācariya) for at least the first five years. If they considered qualified at that point they may be released from dependence. Here the Buddha plays with this idea, inverting the standard meaning.
comment_text.sn35.151:1.6: The Buddha reveals the pun: the defilements “reside within” (anto vasanti) as a “resident pupil” (antevāsi) lives with the teacher.
comment_text.sn35.151:1.8: The defilements “master” (samudācaranti) them like a “teaching master” (ācariya).

root_text.sn35.151:1.2: Santevāsiko, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sācariyako dukkhaṁ na phāsu viharati.
root_text.sn35.151:1.3: Anantevāsiko, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anācariyako sukhaṁ phāsu viharati.
root_text.sn35.151:1.4: Kathañca, bhikkhu, santevāsiko sācariyako dukkhaṁ na phāsu viharati?
root_text.sn35.151:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:1.6: Tyāssa anto vasanti, antassa vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:1.8: Te naṁ samudācaranti, samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:2.2: Tyāssa anto vasanti, antassa vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:2.4: Te naṁ samudācaranti, samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti. Tasmā sācariyakoti vuccati …pe….
root_text.sn35.151:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno manasā dhammaṁ viññāya uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:3.2: Tyāssa anto vasanti, antassa vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:3.4: Te naṁ samudācaranti, samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:3.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu santevāsiko sācariyako dukkhaṁ, na phāsu viharati.
root_text.sn35.151:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anantevāsiko anācariyako sukhaṁ phāsu viharati?
root_text.sn35.151:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā na uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:4.3: Tyāssa na anto vasanti, nāssa anto vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:4.5: Te naṁ na samudācaranti, na samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā na uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:5.2: Tyāssa na anto vasanti, nāssa anto vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:5.4: Te naṁ na samudācaranti, na samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti. Tasmā anācariyakoti vuccati …pe….
root_text.sn35.151:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno manasā dhammaṁ viññāya na uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.151:6.2: Tyāssa na anto vasanti, nāssa anto vasanti pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:6.4: Te naṁ na samudācaranti, na samudācaranti naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammāti.
root_text.sn35.151:6.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anantevāsiko anācariyako sukhaṁ phāsu viharati.
root_text.sn35.151:6.8: Anācariyakaṁ santevāsiko, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sācariyako dukkhaṁ, na phāsu viharati.
root_text.sn35.151:6.9: Anantevāsiko, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anācariyako sukhaṁ phāsu viharatī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.151:1.1: “Mendicants, this spiritual life is lived without a resident pupil and without a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:1.2: A mendicant who lives with a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in suffering and discomfort.
translation_text.sn35.151:1.3: A mendicant who lives without a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in happiness and comfort.
translation_text.sn35.151:1.4: And how does a mendicant who lives with a resident pupil and a teaching master live in suffering and discomfort?
translation_text.sn35.151:1.5: When a mendicant sees a sight with the eye, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.151:1.6: Those qualities reside within. Since they have bad unskillful qualities residing within,
translation_text.sn35.151:1.7: they’re said to have a resident pupil.
translation_text.sn35.151:1.8: Those qualities master them. Since they’re mastered by bad unskillful qualities,
translation_text.sn35.151:1.9: they’re said to have a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:2.1: Furthermore, when a mendicant hears … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.151:3.1: knows an idea with the mind, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.151:3.2: Those qualities reside within. Since they have bad unskillful qualities residing within,
translation_text.sn35.151:3.3: they’re said to have a resident pupil.
translation_text.sn35.151:3.4: Those qualities master them. Since they’re mastered by bad unskillful qualities,
translation_text.sn35.151:3.5: they’re said to have a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:3.6: That’s how a mendicant who lives with a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in suffering and discomfort.
translation_text.sn35.151:4.1: And how does a mendicant who lives without a resident pupil and a teaching master live in happiness and comfort?
translation_text.sn35.151:4.2: When a mendicant sees a sight with the eye, bad, unskillful phenomena don’t arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.151:4.3: Those qualities don’t reside within. Since they don’t have bad unskillful qualities residing within,
translation_text.sn35.151:4.4: they’re said to not have a resident pupil.
translation_text.sn35.151:4.5: Those qualities don’t master them. Since they’re not mastered by bad unskillful qualities,
translation_text.sn35.151:4.6: they’re said to not have a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:5.1: Furthermore, when a mendicant hears … smells … tastes … touches …
translation_text.sn35.151:6.1: knows an idea with the mind, bad, unskillful phenomena don’t arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.2: Those qualities don’t reside within. Since they don’t have bad unskillful qualities residing within,
translation_text.sn35.151:6.3: they’re said to not have a resident pupil.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.4: Those qualities don’t master them. Since they’re not mastered by bad unskillful qualities,
translation_text.sn35.151:6.5: they’re said to not have a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.6: That’s how a mendicant who lives without a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in happiness and comfort.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.7: This spiritual life is lived without a resident pupil and without a teaching master.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.8: A mendicant who lives with a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in suffering and discomfort.
translation_text.sn35.151:6.9: A mendicant who lives without a resident pupil and a teaching master lives in happiness and comfort.”
